---
title: "gaussian_window"
generated: true
description: "Separable Gaussian patch-weight window, peak 1 at the centre."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.numerics.spatial`](../nitrix-numerics-spatial.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gaussian_window -->
```python
gaussian_window(shape: Sequence[int], *, sigma_scale: float = 0.125, dtype: jnp.dtype = jnp.float32) -> Float[Array, '*shape']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gaussian_window -->
Separable Gaussian patch-weight window, peak 1 at the centre.

Builds an `len(shape)`-dimensional window as the outer product of
per-axis unnormalised Gaussian profiles, each centred on its axis and
peaking at 1. Used to down-weight patch borders when blending
overlapping sliding-window predictions, so seams between tiles are
smooth.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gaussian_window -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `shape` | `Sequence[int]` | — | — | Per-axis window size; the returned array has this shape. |
| `sigma_scale` | `float` | — | `0.125` | Per-axis standard deviation as a fraction of the axis size: the standard deviation for an axis of length `size` is $\sigma_{\mathrm{scale}} \cdot \mathrm{size}$ (floored at a small positive value). Defaults to `0.125`. |
| `dtype` | `jnp.dtype` | — | `jnp.float32` | Floating-point dtype of the returned window. Defaults to `jnp.float32`. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `*shape`. The separable Gaussian window with the requested `shape`, valued 1 at the centre and decaying towards the borders.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:gaussian_window -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*shape` | a variadic group of axes (`shape`) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
